test.name: Refuse to declare when plan item is still in progress
test.description: q_001 in flynn-plan-in-progress has pli_005 (death certificate search) still at status 'in_progress'. The SKILL.md explicitly says: 'If items are still in_progress, refuse to declare and recommend completing the in-flight work first.' The skill must refuse and tell the user to finish the death certificate search before evaluating exhaustiveness.

input.user_message: Can we declare the research on q_001 exhaustive? I think we have enough with the two census records.

judge_context: Claude must REFUSE to write an exhaustive declaration because pli_005 (death certificate search) has status 'in_progress'.
Claude should specifically identify pli_005 as the in-progress plan item blocking the declaration.
Claude should recommend completing the death certificate search before evaluating exhaustiveness.
Claude must NOT set q_001.exhaustive_declaration.declared to true or q_001.status to 'exhaustive_declared'.

Claude should NOT write any changes to research.json — the refusal is conversational, not a data write. If Claude does call validate_research_schema that is acceptable but not required since no changes were made.

Since the skill is refusing to declare (no declaration is being written), the Stop criteria coverage dimension should be scored as pass or N/A — a refusal to evaluate does not need to include a formal stop_criteria object. The rubric says 'when the skill writes a declaration (either direction)' — a refusal is neither direction, it is a procedural gate before evaluation begins.
